text: Ferrari's strategy department operates on a strict diet of vibes and panic.
"Plan G" for Ferrari actually stands for "Guess we're finishing 6th."
Mercedes spends millions on aerodynamics just to have Toto Wolff slam his fist through a table.
Red Bull gives you wings; Max Verstappen takes them away from everyone else.
Alpine's biggest rival on the track isn't another team, it's usually the other Alpine.
Haas is an ongoing experiment to see how far you can get in F1 using spare parts and hope.
Williams F1: Proving that deep historical heritage doesn't actually buy you downforce.
Aston Martin's master plan is just hoping Fernando Alonso never ages.
"We are checking" is Maranello for "Please hold while we panic."
Max Verstappen's idea of a close race is winning by only 20 seconds.
Lewis Hamilton has more comebacks than a 90s boy band reunion tour.
Lando Norris races like he's late for a dentist appointment he doesn't want to go to.
Charles Leclerc's radio messages sound like a man slowly losing faith in humanity.
Fernando Alonso has been racing so long he once overtook a horse-drawn carriage.
Monaco GP: Where billionaires pay thousands to watch cars they can't overtake each other.
Spa-Francorchamps: The only place where it rains on one corner and is sunny on the next.
Singapore GP exists purely to test whether F1 drivers can sweat more than they weigh.
Monza: Where slipstreaming is less a tactic and more a lifestyle.
DRS stands for 'Drag Reduction System' but really means 'Drama Removal System'.
F1 steering wheels cost more than most people's cars and have more buttons than a spaceship.
A pit stop takes 2 seconds. Finding your car keys in the morning takes 10 minutes.
The F1 paddock has more drama than a reality TV show and better costumes.
Team principals are basically football managers but with more expensive tantrums.
Safety car: The only car on track that makes everyone equally angry.
Blue flags are F1's way of saying 'Excuse me sir, you're in the way'.
